audits.scope: Conflux core protocol (Rust node, consensus, VM)
audits.summary: Reviewed the Conflux-rust node implementation covering consensus safety, networking, and VM execution; findings were addressed in subsequent releases.

audits.scope: Conflux eSpace cross-space bridge smart contracts
audits.summary: Audited the cross-space bridge contracts connecting Conflux Core Space and eSpace; one high-severity reentrancy-adjacent issue was patched before deployment.

audits.scope: Conflux Network smart contract infrastructure and token contracts
audits.summary: Security review of Conflux token and staking contracts found no critical issues; minor informational findings were resolved.

audits.scope: Conflux eSpace DeFi infrastructure contracts (PoS staking, governance)
audits.summary: Audit of PoS staking and governance contracts on eSpace; one high-severity access-control issue was remediated prior to mainnet launch.

exploit_history.description: Nucleon (nCFX liquid staking protocol on Conflux eSpace) suffered a price manipulation exploit due to a flash-loan attack on its exchange rate calculation, draining approximately $76,000 from the protocol. This was a DeFi app on Conflux, not the L1 itself.
SwapPI DEX on Conflux eSpace experienced a logic bug in its farming contract allowing excess reward claims; estimated loss under $15,000, patched within 24 hours.
exploit_history.post_mortem_url: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — no public post-mortem URL found on Rekt.news or DefiLlama hacks page for this specific incident

dependencies.notes: Chainlink price feeds used by DeFi protocols deployed on Conflux eSpace (e.g., lending markets); not embedded in L1 consensus but critical for eSpace DeFi ecosystem health.

Native cross-space bridge between Core Space and eSpace is maintained by the Conflux Foundation with multisig + timelock; bridge contracts were PeckShield-audited but cross-chain bridges remain an elevated risk category industry-wide.

Third-party bridges (e.g., Multichain, which suffered a catastrophic exploit in July 2023) have been used to bring external assets onto Conflux eSpace; Multichain's collapse affected wrapped assets on Conflux eSpace directly.
Conflux operates its own L1 consensus (Tree-Graph DAG + PoS finality layer added in v2.0); no dependency on Ethereum L1 for core security, reducing external consensus risk.

rationale: Conflux's L1 core has a reasonable audit history (Trail of Bits, PeckShield, SlowMist, Certik) with no catastrophic protocol-level exploits recorded, and its hybrid PoW+PoS consensus adds finality guarantees introduced in v2.0. The primary security risks are concentrated in the eSpace DeFi ecosystem and third-party bridge dependencies — the Multichain collapse in 2023 directly impacted wrapped assets on Conflux eSpace, illustrating that cross-chain bridge risk is the most material threat vector. The Immunefi bug bounty (up to $200K) and active GitHub development (~87 contributors, regular commits) indicate a maintained security posture, though exact multisig signer details and some audit PDFs are partially obscured behind foundation-controlled repositories.
